title: Replace token-path webview relay with dedicated host ports per sandbox app
description:
The current sandbox app origin relay multiplexes all host webview traffic through one host port using `/t/<relay-token>/...` paths. That makes ordinary web apps break unless they are path-prefix safe, because root-absolute asset and API URLs like `/styles.css`, `/script.js`, `/submit`, and `/media?...` escape the relay prefix.

Switch the relay contract to dedicate one random host localhost port per registered sandbox app instead.

New target model:
- sandbox app listens on `127.0.0.1:<sandbox-port>`
- AGS allocates one random host port for that registration
- `ags-webview-url <sandbox-port> [base_path]` returns a host URL rooted at `/`, e.g. `http://127.0.0.1:43126/` or `http://127.0.0.1:43126/app`
- AGS stores a simple map `host_port -> AGS session -> sandbox local port/base_path`
- root-absolute app URLs work unchanged because `/` is once again the app root

Success criteria:
- no `/t/<token>/...` requirement for the primary served-app flow
- an interview-style app with root-absolute asset/API/media URLs works without HTML/JS path-prefix patches
- AGS still preserves session scoping, randomization, cleanup, and least-privilege boundaries
- Glimpse callers keep the same `resolveSandboxAppURL()` / `openURL()` flow
close_reason: Dedicated-port webview relay implemented across AGS, helper, shim, docs, and tests

title: [design] Revise the sandbox app relay contract for dedicated host origins
description:
Update the host UI / sandbox app relay RFC to replace shared-port path multiplexing with one dedicated host origin per registered sandbox app.

Specify precisely:
- why host port is sufficient routing information and relay tokens are no longer needed for the primary flow
- the new helper contract: `ags-webview-url <port> [base_path] -> http://127.0.0.1:<host-port>/...`
- whether `base_path` is still prepended inside the sandbox or encoded directly in the returned URL
- lifecycle and cleanup rules for host listener allocation per registration
- session ownership and how AGS prevents one session from reaching another session's relayed port
- whether any temporary compatibility mode for `/t/<token>/...` remains during migration
- exact security boundaries vs generic host/container TCP forwarding

Deliverable: an updated written contract that downstream implementation can follow without guessing.
close_reason: Updated relay contract docs for dedicated host origins and per-app host ports

title: [validation] Verify pi-interview-tool works unchanged once Glimpse owns localhost relay resolution
description:
Validate the architecture on a real Glimpse-using package without relying on package-specific AGS relay patches.

Target scenario:
- `pi-interview-tool` constructs its normal localhost URL for its sandbox-local server
- it hands that URL to Glimpse
- Glimpse performs the localhost-to-host relay conversion automatically
- interview frontend files stay in their original root-absolute form (`/styles.css`, `/script.js`, `/submit`, `/media?...`)

Acceptance criteria:
- no raw host attempt to connect to `localhost:<sandbox-port>`
- no package-owned `AGS_WEBVIEW_URL_HELPER` / `AGS_WEBVIEW_RELAY_SOCKET` logic is needed for the standard path
- the interview app opens in Glimpse and loads successfully with root-absolute URLs unchanged
- capture exact before/after behavior so future Glimpse-using packages can rely on the same contract
close_reason: Validated pi-interview-tool now passes ordinary localhost URLs to Glimpse with no package-owned AGS relay logic

title: [linux/glimpse] Use normal toplevel windows for standard app UIs; reserve layer-shell for overlay modes
description:
Fix the Linux Glimpse backend so ordinary app/dialog windows behave like normal desktop windows instead of always being immovable fixed-size overlay surfaces.

Current observed behavior:
- Glimpse windows on Linux cannot be moved normally and appear fixed-size
- implementation reason: `../glimpse/src/linux/src/main.rs` currently initializes `gtk4_layer_shell` unconditionally (`window.init_layer_shell()`, overlay layer, top/left anchoring), which is appropriate for overlays/HUDs but not for standard app windows such as interview forms
- size is set once via `window.set_default_size(args.width, args.height)` and the backend does not appear to expose normal desktop-window behavior for the common non-overlay case

Required architectural change:
- split Linux window creation into at least two modes:
  1. normal desktop toplevel window mode for standard Glimpse windows / served apps / dialogs
  2. layer-shell overlay mode only when required for overlay semantics
- standard windows should be movable/resizable/decorated according to normal desktop behavior unless explicitly disabled by options
- layer-shell should remain available for explicit overlay-style cases such as follow-cursor, click-through overlays, transparent companions, or other always-on-top HUD use cases

Scope:
- inspect `../glimpse/src/linux/src/main.rs` and any related backend bridge/protocol code
- define the condition for choosing normal window mode vs overlay mode
- preserve existing overlay-only capabilities where they actually depend on layer-shell
- ensure standard served-app windows like interview do not lose expected functionality while overlay widgets still work

Acceptance criteria:
- on Linux, a standard `open()` / `openURL()` Glimpse window opens as a normal desktop window that can be moved and resized
- overlay-specific options still use the appropriate layer-shell path when needed
- the behavior is documented so package authors know which options trigger overlay semantics
- any tests/docs updated to reflect the split behavior

title: [ags] Allocate one host listener per relayed sandbox app and route by host port
description:
Rework the AGS webview relay implementation so registration allocates a dedicated random host localhost port for that sandbox app.

Scope:
- replace the single shared host listener + `/t/<token>/...` router as the default served-app path
- maintain a session-scoped in-memory map from `host_port` to sandbox-local destination (`sandbox_port`, optional `base_path`, AGS session/runtime dir)
- bind each listener only on host loopback
- forward HTTP requests for that listener to the sandbox relay shim without requiring a path token
- clean up all allocated listeners when the AGS session exits or the registration is removed
- preserve clear host-side diagnostics for listener allocation failures, collisions, and cleanup

Acceptance criteria:
- a request to `http://127.0.0.1:<allocated-port>/styles.css` reaches the correct sandbox app without any token/path prefix
- no broad host-to-container networking is exposed beyond the dedicated listeners AGS owns
close_reason: Reworked AGS relay to allocate one host listener per registered sandbox app

title: [ags] Update the sandbox relay shim and ags-webview-url helper for dedicated-origin registration
description:
Update the sandbox-side pieces to match the new dedicated-origin contract.

Scope:
- revise the Unix-socket registration protocol so the sandbox helper can request a relayed app and receive the dedicated host origin details
- make `ags-webview-url` print the final host URL rooted at `/`, not a `/t/<token>/...` path
- keep support for optional `base_path` routing without corrupting query strings
- ensure the shim no longer depends on path-token extraction for normal HTTP forwarding
- verify that app-level query params like `?session=...` remain intact end-to-end

Acceptance criteria:
- helper output can be handed directly to `openURL()`
- a root request plus later requests to `/script.js`, `/submit`, `/media?...`, etc. all resolve against the same relayed app without path rewriting
close_reason: Updated sandbox shim and ags-webview-url helper for dedicated-origin registration

title: [compat] Prove interview-style root-absolute apps work unchanged over the relay
description:
Add compatibility coverage using an interview-style temporary web app that assumes it lives at origin root.

Cover at minimum:
- HTML with root-absolute asset URLs (`/styles.css`, `/script.js`)
- JS calls to root-absolute endpoints (`/heartbeat`, `/cancel`, `/submit`, `/save`, `/sessions`)
- image/media loading via root-absolute URLs with query parameters (`/media?path=...&session=...`)
- app-level query params used for auth/session validation surviving all requests intact

This task should demonstrate that the relay works for existing localhost-style apps without requiring relative-URL patches, `<base>` hacks, or injected fetch monkeypatches.
close_reason: Added coverage proving root-absolute interview-style app requests work unchanged

title: [docs/testing] Update relay docs, migration notes, and automated coverage for the dedicated-origin model
description:
Finish the migration by updating documentation and automated coverage across the contract boundary.

Scope:
- update `docs/GLIMPSE_SANDBOX_APP_ORIGIN_RELAY.md` and `docs/GLIMPSE_HOST_UI_BRIDGE.md` to describe dedicated host origins/ports
- document explicitly that the primary served-app flow no longer requires path-prefix-safe apps
- add AGS tests for listener allocation, cleanup, and correct routing by host port
- add negative tests for session isolation and stale-listener cleanup
- if a compatibility fallback for `/t/<token>/...` is kept, document when it is used and when it can be removed

Acceptance criteria:
- the docs match the implementation exactly
- tests prove root-absolute URL compatibility and teardown behavior
close_reason: Updated docs and automated coverage for the dedicated-origin relay model

title: [integration] Align pi-interview-tool with the dedicated-port AGS webview relay contract
description:
Fix the installed/upstream `pi-interview-tool` integration so it works with the current AGS dedicated-host-port webview relay **without** any frontend path-prefix patches.

Current observed failure:
- interview opens in Glimpse, but after reverting the relative-path workaround it shows a white screen with `Could not connect to localhost: Connection refused`
- root cause: `server.ts` still gates relay usage on `process.env.AGS_WEBVIEW_RELAY_BASE_URL`, but AGS no longer exports that variable
- AGS now exposes the relay via `AGS_WEBVIEW_RELAY_SOCKET` and `AGS_WEBVIEW_URL_HELPER`
- because the old env var is absent, interview falls back to `http://localhost:<sandbox-port>/?session=...`
- that URL points at sandbox-local localhost, which the host-owned Glimpse webview cannot reach, so the host connection fails
- there is also a helper-contract bug: interview passes `/?session=...` as the helper's second argument, but `ags-webview-url <port> [base_path]` expects a base path, not a path+query string blob

Files to inspect/fix:
- `/home/tom/.pi/agent/git/github.com/nicobailon/pi-interview-tool/server.ts`
- likely the upstream copy of the same repo if this should be persisted upstream
- AGS contract references:
  - `agent/webview-url-helper`
  - `crates/ags/src/webview_relay.rs`
  - `crates/ags/src/plan/build.rs`

Required behavior:
- keep the Glimpse discovery fix in `index.ts` (the `~/.pi/agent/extensions/glimpseui/src/glimpse.mjs` lookup)
- do **not** require the earlier relative-URL/frontend path patches in `form/index.html` or `form/script.js`
- `resolveInterviewUrl()` must detect the relay using the current AGS contract (`AGS_WEBVIEW_URL_HELPER` and/or `AGS_WEBVIEW_RELAY_SOCKET`)
- it must call the helper with a real base path, normally `/`
- it must append interview's `session` query param after the helper returns the dedicated host URL
- final Glimpse-opened URL should be host-reachable, e.g. `http://127.0.0.1:<host-port>/?session=...`, not `http://localhost:<sandbox-port>/...`
- root-absolute asset/API/media URLs (`/styles.css`, `/script.js`, `/submit`, `/media?...`) must work unchanged over the dedicated-port relay

Acceptance criteria:
- with frontend files left in their original root-absolute form, interview opens in Glimpse and loads normally
- no `Connection refused` to raw `localhost:<sandbox-port>` on the host
- no `Invalid session: /tmp/pi-clipboard-....png` regression caused by mangling query/path handling
- the fix is captured in a bead summary with exact before/after behavior and any upstream follow-up needed
close_reason: Updated pi-interview-tool to detect current AGS relay env and append session query after helper-resolved host URL; added tests

title: [glimpse] Auto-resolve sandbox localhost URLs through the AGS relay before opening host webviews
description:
Move sandbox-local web app reachability into `glimpseui` instead of teaching each package about AGS relay internals.

Problem statement:
- packages that depend on `glimpseui` should be able to call `glimpse.open("http://localhost:<port>/...")` or the equivalent prompt flow and have it work in AGS automatically
- package authors should not need to know about `AGS_WEBVIEW_URL_HELPER`, `AGS_WEBVIEW_RELAY_SOCKET`, relay tokens, host ports, or URL rewriting
- per-package patches (like custom `resolveInterviewUrl()` logic in `pi-interview-tool`) do not scale and are the wrong architecture boundary

Implement in Glimpse:
- detect sandbox-local URLs passed to the host-open path: at minimum `http://localhost:<port>/...` and `http://127.0.0.1:<port>/...`
- when AGS webview relay support is available, call `ags-webview-url <port> /` to obtain the dedicated host origin for that sandbox app
- reconstruct the final host URL by preserving the original request path, query string, and hash exactly
- if the input URL is not sandbox-local localhost, leave it unchanged
- keep the existing high-level Glimpse API surface (`open()`, `prompt()`, and any current caller contract) intact

Acceptance criteria:
- a package can pass `http://localhost:<sandbox-port>/?session=abc` to Glimpse and Glimpse opens a host-reachable URL like `http://127.0.0.1:<host-port>/?session=abc`
- no package-specific AGS env handling is required for the common served-app flow
- the dedicated-port relay remains the only AGS-specific dependency; callers still just pass URLs
close_reason: Glimpse now auto-resolves sandbox localhost URLs through the AGS relay before host open

title: [glimpse/tests] Cover localhost URL rewriting and exact path/query/hash preservation
description:
Add focused Glimpse tests for the new localhost auto-relay behavior.

Cover at minimum:
- `http://localhost:<port>/`
- `http://localhost:<port>/?session=abc`
- `http://localhost:<port>/app/index.html?x=1#frag`
- `http://127.0.0.1:<port>/media?path=/tmp/pi-clipboard-test.png&session=abc`
- non-localhost URLs are passed through unchanged
- behavior when the AGS relay helper/socket is unavailable falls back cleanly or emits an explicit actionable error, whichever contract Glimpse adopts

Tests must prove that:
- the helper is called with a real base path, not a path+query blob
- query strings are not folded into the path
- clipboard/media-style query params survive unchanged end-to-end
- no path-prefix workarounds or HTML `<base>` rewriting are required for root-absolute app URLs once the dedicated-port relay URL is opened
close_reason: Added Glimpse tests for localhost rewriting, exact path/query/hash preservation, and fallback behavior

title: [contract/docs] Make Glimpse the sole owner of sandbox-local URL resolution for packages
description:
Update the architecture/docs to define the correct ownership boundary:

- packages/tools own their normal localhost app URL construction (for example `http://localhost:<port>/?session=...`)
- `glimpseui` owns converting sandbox-local localhost URLs into host-reachable AGS relay URLs when running under AGS
- AGS owns dedicated host-port allocation and HTTP forwarding for those converted URLs
- packages should not read AGS relay env vars directly or call `ags-webview-url` themselves for the standard Glimpse-served-app flow

Include exact guidance for package authors:
- if you use Glimpse, pass the ordinary app URL to Glimpse and let it resolve transport details
- do not build `/t/<token>/...` URLs yourself
- do not rely on path-prefix-safe frontend hacks as the compatibility mechanism

Acceptance criteria:
- docs clearly state that package-specific relay logic is discouraged/superseded
- the architecture matches the intended dedicated-port relay model and the actual Glimpse integration point
close_reason: Updated docs to make Glimpse the owner of sandbox-local URL resolution for packages

title: Refine Pi guard bash sensitive-path checks to avoid inert-string false positives
description:
Goal
Tighten or remove the remaining Pi guard bash sensitive-path string heuristic in `agent/extensions/guard.ts` so AGS does not block inert strings while still preserving sandbox-specific protections where they add value beyond dcg.

Scope
- Re-evaluate whether Pi should inspect bash command strings for sensitive paths at all
- If path checks remain, replace raw substring matching with a narrower approach and add regression tests
- Keep dcg as the primary bash classifier
- Document the behavior if user-facing docs need clarification

Acceptance Criteria
- Pi no longer blocks bash commands merely because a sensitive path appears in inert text
- Remaining Pi-owned bash checks, if any, are clearly scoped to sandbox-specific protections
- Tests cover the chosen behavior
notes:
Started follow-up refinement on Pi guard.ts. Plan: remove the remaining raw bash sensitive-path substring heuristic so Pi guard owns only structured sandbox boundary checks plus dcg delegation, then add regression coverage proving inert path strings in bash commands are not blocked by AGS.

Implementation summary
- Removed the remaining Pi bash sensitive-path substring block from `agent/extensions/guard.ts`.
- Pi now keeps structured file-tool boundary checks (read/write roots, sensitive files, secret-like write targets) and delegates bash classification entirely to dcg.
- Extended guard regression coverage to assert the Pi extension no longer contains AGS bash string heuristics such as `command.includes(...)` or the old sensitive-path denial reason.
- Validated with `cargo test -p ags --test guard_hook -- --nocapture`.
close_reason: Removed the remaining Pi bash sensitive-path substring heuristic from guard.ts, leaving only structured sandbox boundary checks plus dcg delegation, and added regression coverage proving AGS no longer uses raw bash string heuristics there.

title: Investigate and integrate destructive_command_guard into sandbox clankers
description:
Goal
Integrate destructive_command_guard (DCG) into sandbox command execution paths ("clankers") with a safe, practical policy configuration.

Reference
https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/destructive_command_guard

Scope
- Audit all clanker execution entry points where DCG should be injected
- Evaluate integration modes (wrapper, middleware, pre-exec check)
- Propose default policy and override mechanisms
- Implement integration for selected clankers
- Document policy tuning and developer workflow impacts

Acceptance Criteria
- Target clankers pass commands through DCG checks
- Default policy is defined and version-controlled
- Behavior for blocked commands is clear (message, logs, override path)
- Integration docs include how to tune policy and test blocked/allowed commands

Definition of Done
- Destructive command protection is active in agreed clanker paths with documented, testable configuration
notes:
Investigation notes
- Current AGS guard code is only partially useful: path/sensitive-file/root-boundary checks are valuable, but Bash pattern checks are too noisy and should not duplicate policy that belongs in destructive_command_guard.
- Known false-positive example: the current Bash guard blocks benign commands containing words like `shutdown` in commit messages or other string data. In the sandbox, many host-power commands are also operationally irrelevant.
- Direction: AGS-owned guards should be narrowed to sandbox-specific gaps that dcg does not cover well (e.g. sensitive-path access and read/write boundary enforcement), while dcg becomes the primary Bash command classifier.
- Provisioning requirement: if AGS integrates dcg, dcg must be available inside the sandbox image and refreshed by AGS update flows.
- Config direction: there is no implicit sandbox-global dcg config today. Preferred override story is host-managed config mounted into the sandbox when desired, plus project-local `.dcg.toml` in the workspace.

Implementation summary
- Pi guard extension now keeps sandbox-specific path/read/write protections and delegates Bash classification to dcg rather than maintaining an AGS regex denylist.
- Claude guard hook now keeps sandbox-boundary checks and passes original Bash hook payloads through to dcg so Claude-compatible hook output is preserved.
- The sandbox image now bundles dcg from release artifacts with checksum verification, and `ags update` refreshes dcg alongside br/bv.
- Config/docs now describe the preferred host-mounted dcg override model (`~/.config/dcg`, optional `~/.local/share/dcg`) plus a recommended starting pack baseline of `database.postgresql` and `containers.docker` with core protections implicit.
- Validation completed via `cargo test -p ags`, targeted guard-hook regression tests, and a local Podman image build/run verifying `br`, `bv`, and `dcg` inside the sandbox image.
close_reason: Integrated dcg into AGS sandbox clankers: refined Pi/Claude guards to cover sandbox-only gaps, bundled dcg in the sandbox image/update flow, documented host-mounted config overrides and default pack baseline, and validated with tests plus a successful Podman build/run.

title: Investigate Pi integration path for destructive_command_guard
description:
Goal
Map the cleanest integration path for destructive_command_guard in AGS Pi runs before implementation.

Scope
- Audit current Pi guard extension flow (extension install, launch args, env, runtime UX)
- Decide whether Pi should keep AGS-owned prechecks plus optional dcg shell-out, or move to a thinner dcg-first model
- Validate how dcg agent detection behaves under Pi in the sandbox and whether AGS should pass explicit hints/env
- Define how blocked-command feedback should appear in Pi UI and logs
- List tests needed for safe rollout

Acceptance Criteria
- Clear recommendation for Pi integration mode and hook ordering
- Identified config knobs needed for Pi-specific behavior
- Test plan covers blocked and allowed command paths
notes:
Investigation notes
- Pi extension model is a good fit for AGS-owned sandbox gap checks: `tool_call` hooks can block specific built-in tools before execution, and `pi.exec()` can shell out to helper binaries like dcg from the extension runtime.
- Current AGS Pi guard reproduces obvious false positives because it applies naive regexes to the raw Bash command string. Example reproduced locally: `git commit -m "docs: mention shutdown behavior"` matches the current `\b(shutdown|reboot|poweroff|halt)\b` rule and would be blocked even though it is just commit-message text.
- Recommendation direction: for Pi, keep the extension as the place for sandbox-specific path/read/write/sensitive-file checks and UI status, but remove broad Bash heuristics and let dcg classify shell commands.
- Pi-specific open question remains agent identity: dcg docs/source do not show first-class Pi detection today, so Pi likely evaluates under the `unknown` profile unless AGS passes an explicit override or dcg gains Pi support upstream.

Implementation progress
- Removed the AGS Bash regex denylist from the Pi guard extension; it now keeps sandbox-gap protections and delegates Bash classification to dcg.
- Added a regression test to ensure the Pi guard no longer carries the old shutdown regex and still routes Bash evaluation through dcg.
close_reason: Completed Pi integration investigation: AGS should keep only sandbox-specific path/read/write protections in the Pi extension, delegate Bash classification to dcg, and treat Pi as an unknown dcg agent profile unless upstream/explicit detection is added later. The Pi guard implementation and regression coverage were updated accordingly.

title: Investigate Claude integration path for destructive_command_guard
description:
Goal
After Pi, map the cleanest integration path for destructive_command_guard in AGS Claude runs.

Scope
- Audit current Claude PreToolUse hook and plugin-dir setup in AGS
- Decide whether Claude should keep AGS-owned path/sensitive-file checks in guard.sh before dcg, wrap dcg directly, or delegate more logic to dcg
- Verify Claude hook input/output/exit-code semantics remain compatible with dcg fail-open behavior
- Identify any settings-merge or plugin-dir concerns for user overrides
- List tests needed for safe rollout

Acceptance Criteria
- Clear recommendation for Claude integration mode and hook ordering
- Identified config knobs needed for Claude-specific behavior
- Test plan covers blocked and allowed command paths
notes:
Investigation notes
- Claude hook mode in dcg is richer than `dcg test`: when invoked as a hook with the original stdin JSON, dcg returns Claude-compatible `hookSpecificOutput` JSON on stdout for denies while still exiting 0 on normal allow/deny decisions.
- That suggests the best Claude integration is probably a thin AGS wrapper: keep sandbox-gap checks in `guard.sh`, but for Bash hand the original hook payload through to dcg rather than re-implementing shell classification with AGS regexes or downgrading to `dcg test`.
- Non-Bash tools should remain handled by AGS guard logic only (path/read/write/sensitive-file boundaries).
- This would preserve Claude-specific remediation/rule metadata from dcg and remove the current false-positive-prone Bash heuristics.
close_reason: Completed Claude integration investigation: AGS should keep sandbox-boundary checks in guard.sh and hand the original Bash hook payload through to dcg so Claude-specific hook JSON and remediation metadata are preserved. The Claude guard implementation and regression coverage were updated accordingly.

title: Design default dcg policy and AGS override strategy
description:
Goal
Define the default destructive_command_guard policy for AGS and the override story across dcg global config and per-project config.

Scope
- Evaluate which packs should be enabled by default for sandbox agents
- Decide whether AGS should rely on dcg native layering (~/.config/dcg/config.toml, .dcg.toml, DCG_CONFIG/env overrides) or introduce AGS-owned config fields for generated defaults
- Define precedence between AGS defaults, user global dcg config, project-local .dcg.toml, and runtime env overrides
- Document where version-controlled defaults should live and how operators opt out or tune behavior
- Recommend warn-vs-deny posture for rollout and developer workflow impact

Acceptance Criteria
- Recommended default pack/policy baseline for AGS
- Clear precedence model for global and project overrides
- Migration notes for implementation and documentation work
notes:
Investigation notes
- There is no sandbox-global dcg config by default today.
- Preferred configuration model: use dcg's native layering, with host-managed global config mounted into the sandbox when operators want one, and `.dcg.toml` in the project repo for project-local overrides.
- Avoid inventing a parallel AGS policy schema unless a narrow AGS-specific need remains after the integration design is settled.
- Need to define/document the recommended mount path for user-provided dcg config.
- Additional nuance: some dcg features also use user-state paths outside `config.toml` (for example pending allow-once exceptions in `~/.config/dcg/` and history in `~/.local/share/dcg/`), so we should decide whether AGS documents/mounts just config, or the broader dcg user-state directories when those workflows are desired.
- Recommended host-side baseline when users opt in to global dcg config: keep core protections implicit and enable `database.postgresql` plus `containers.docker` as the starting pack set.

Implementation progress
- Documented the preferred host-mount model in `config/config.example.toml` and `docs/CONFIG.md` using optional `~/.config/dcg` and `~/.local/share/dcg` mounts.
- Documented a recommended host-side dcg baseline using `database.postgresql` and `containers.docker` without adding AGS-specific policy schema.
- Kept AGS defaults implicit: dcg works in the sandbox without a mounted global config, but host-managed global config/state can be opted in explicitly.
close_reason: Defined the AGS dcg config/override strategy: no implicit sandbox-global config, prefer host-mounted ~/.config/dcg and ~/.local/share/dcg when desired, rely on project-local .dcg.toml for repo overrides, and recommend database.postgresql + containers.docker as the host-side starting baseline with core protections implicit.

title: Decide how AGS provisions dcg into the sandbox runtime
description:
Goal
Choose the lowest-friction, most reliable way to make destructive_command_guard available inside AGS sandboxes.

Scope
- Compare bundling dcg into the base image vs installing/updating it via AGS commands vs mounting a host binary as a tool
- Evaluate version pinning, update cadence, offline behavior, and architecture support
- Decide what doctor/setup/update should verify or self-heal
- Identify tests and docs needed so Pi/Claude integrations do not silently fail open because dcg is missing
- Include the requirement that dcg be present in the sandbox Containerfile if AGS adopts dcg as part of the guard stack
- Define how AGS update flows keep dcg current

Acceptance Criteria
- Recommended provisioning/update path for dcg in AGS
- Versioning and verification plan is documented
- Follow-on implementation touchpoints include Containerfile/update/doctor docs and tests
notes:
Current leaning
- If dcg is part of the integrated guard story, it should not be optional-by-accident. It needs an explicit provisioning path, with the base image / update flow being the leading candidate.

Investigation notes
- The latest dcg GitHub release currently exposes prebuilt Linux assets such as `dcg-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.xz` and `dcg-aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.xz`, plus checksums and install scripts. This makes Containerfile-based installation via release downloads feasible and aligned with how AGS already provisions br/bv.

Implementation progress
- Added dcg installation to the sandbox Containerfile using release assets + checksum verification.
- Extended `ags update` to resolve and pass a `DCG_VERSION` build arg alongside br/bv and updated its verification output.
- Updated runtime docs/troubleshooting to treat dcg as a bundled sandbox binary verified via `ags update`.
close_reason: Implemented dcg provisioning in the sandbox image and update flow: Containerfile now installs dcg from release assets with checksum verification, ags update resolves/passes DCG_VERSION, config/docs cover optional host-mounted dcg state, and tests/docs were updated for bundled dcg verification.

title: Refine AGS guard layers so they only cover sandbox gaps beyond dcg
description:
Goal
Implement AGS-owned guards so they complement destructive_command_guard instead of duplicating or second-guessing it.

Problem
The current Bash guard logic is noisy and produces false positives (for example matching words like `shutdown` inside commit messages). Some blocked commands are also irrelevant inside the sandbox.

Scope
- Remove or narrow broad Bash-pattern heuristics in Pi/Claude AGS guards
- Keep sandbox-specific protections that dcg does not cover well, especially sensitive-path access and read/write boundary enforcement
- Ensure dcg remains the primary classifier for destructive shell commands
- Align Pi and Claude behavior as much as practical while respecting their different hook APIs
- Add regression tests for known false positives and for sandbox-gap protections that remain in AGS

Acceptance Criteria
- AGS guards no longer block benign Bash commands due to naive string matching
- Remaining AGS guard checks are clearly scoped to sandbox-only protections
- Pi and Claude both preserve useful sensitive-path/root-boundary protections
- Tests cover prior false positives and intended remaining denials
notes:
Investigation notes
- Reproduced current false-positive class: the guard's naive Bash regex for `shutdown|reboot|poweroff|halt` matches harmless command strings like `git commit -m "docs: mention shutdown behavior"`.
- This reinforces the design goal that AGS guard layers should stop doing broad semantic Bash classification and instead focus on sandbox-only protections not covered by dcg.

Implementation progress
- Removed the AGS Bash regex denylist from the Pi guard extension so Bash classification now falls through to dcg (while keeping AGS-specific sensitive-path/read/write protections).
- Reworked the Claude guard hook to keep sandbox boundary checks but delegate Bash evaluation to dcg using the original hook payload, preserving dcg hook JSON when it denies and failing open if dcg itself errors.
- Added regression/integration tests for the Claude hook covering the shutdown false positive, root-boundary checks, sensitive-path checks, dcg passthrough, and fail-open runtime errors.
close_reason: Implemented AGS guard refinement: removed naive Bash denylist from Pi/Claude guard layers, delegated Bash classification to dcg, preserved sandbox boundary checks, and added regression/integration tests for false positives and remaining denials.

title: Bundle br/bv release binaries in sandbox image and refresh via ags update
description:
Goal
Bundle the beads toolchain binaries inside the sandbox image so agents can use them from inside the container exactly like on the host.

Target binaries
- br (beads_rust CLI)
- bv (beads_viewer CLI)

Release sources
- beads_rust (br): https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust/releases
- beads_viewer (bv): https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer/releases

Scope
- Add image build/install logic to fetch/install br and bv from official GitHub releases
- Ensure both binaries are on PATH in the sandbox image
- Wire ags update to refresh installed br/bv to the latest supported release versions
- Add deterministic version verification commands for both tools
- Document failure modes (offline, missing release asset, checksum/permission issues)

Acceptance Criteria
- Fresh sandbox container has working br and bv commands on PATH
- Running ags update refreshes br and bv to the latest supported release versions
- Version verification for br and bv is documented and testable
- Failure behavior is explicit and actionable

Definition of Done
Agents running inside the sandbox can reliably run br/bv without host mounts, and operators can refresh those binaries deterministically via ags update.
close_reason: Implemented br/bv release bundling in sandbox image; ags update now resolves latest beads_rust/beads_viewer tags, rebuilds with build args, and installs verified binaries. Added docs and verification command. Smoke-tested: br 0.1.24, bv v0.15.2 inside sandbox.

title: Set up headless browser testing in sandbox using Playwright
description:
Goal
Enable headless browser tests in sandbox using Playwright (preferred stack).

Question to resolve
Clarify whether setup differs materially in sandbox/container context vs local usage.

Scope
- Define required system dependencies for Playwright in sandbox image
- Add minimal smoke test exercising browser launch and one page interaction
- Verify execution in non-interactive/containerized environment
- Document setup differences, if any (fonts, sandbox flags, deps, CI behavior)

Acceptance Criteria
- Playwright test command runs successfully in sandbox
- Required dependencies are codified in container/build config
- A short doc explains container-specific setup and known limitations
- Recommendation given for next-step expansion (e2e/visual/etc.)

Definition of Done
- Team can reliably run at least one Playwright headless smoke test from within sandbox container

title: Document notification proxy security boundaries and operator setup
description: Document how the host notification proxy works, why the host DBus session bus is not mounted, socket path expectations, permission model, and how to troubleshoot notification delivery.

title: Add psql command in container for Postgres access
description: Add a documented and/or scripted psql command that can be run from inside the container to connect to the project Postgres instance.
close_reason: Completed: added psql client to sandbox image and documented host Postgres quick-connect command.

title: Investigate Testcontainers support for integration tests inside sandbox agent container
description:
Goal
Enable an agent running inside the sandbox container to execute integration tests that rely on Testcontainers.

Context
Current feasibility is unknown (Docker-in-Podman / container runtime access constraints).

Scope
- Investigate runtime options for Testcontainers from inside sandbox container
- Build a minimal proof-of-concept integration test
- Document required container privileges, mounts, env vars, and security trade-offs
- Recommend a supported approach (or explicit non-support decision)

Acceptance Criteria
- A written feasibility report is added to docs/ with recommended approach
- PoC test is runnable via documented command
- Any required container image / compose changes are identified
- Risks and limitations are clearly documented

Definition of Done
- Team can decide Go/No-Go for full Testcontainers adoption based on documented evidence
close_reason: Investigation concluded: pursue standalone PSP project (../podman-socket-proxy, epic psp-287) and AGS integration epic agent-sandbox-3jq

title: Add host-side notification proxy for sandboxed agents
description: Provide a narrow desktop notification channel from sandboxed containers to the host user session without mounting the host DBus session bus. Scope: host-side proxy reachable over a dedicated mounted Unix socket; strict one-way notify-only protocol; explicit security boundaries.

title: Integrate external PSP (podman-socket-proxy) into AGS
description:
Objective
Integrate the standalone PSP project into AGS with minimal code ownership in this repository.

Scope in this repo
- AGS configuration and launch wiring for PSP
- Session lifecycle hooks and connectivity plumbing
- Integration tests validating AGS <-> PSP contract
- User-facing docs for enabling PSP mode in AGS

Out of Scope
- PSP broker internals (API proxy, policy engine, cleanup internals)

Dependency
Requires PSP project deliverables tracked in ../podman-socket-proxy (epic psp-287).
close_reason: done

title: Add AGS config and run wiring for external PSP endpoint
description:
Goal
Allow AGS runs to target PSP via explicit opt-in config/flags.

Acceptance Criteria
- AGS can inject DOCKER_HOST for sandbox sessions in PSP mode
- No direct host Podman socket mount in PSP mode
- Clear errors for missing/unreachable PSP endpoint
notes: Blocked for now while proxy work is deferred pending follow-up.
close_reason: Implemented --psp flag with PspGuard sidecar, socket mount, DOCKER_HOST injection, and shared util extraction

title: Implement AGS-managed PSP session lifecycle hooks
description:
Goal
Coordinate AGS session metadata and PSP lifecycle expectations.

Acceptance Criteria
- AGS passes stable session identifiers/labels expected by PSP
- Normal exit and failure paths invoke cleanup hooks/notifications correctly
- Debug keep-resources mode behavior is explicit in AGS UX
close_reason: done

title: Add AGS integration tests for PSP contract
description:
Goal
Validate AGS<->PSP integration without owning PSP internals.

Acceptance Criteria
- Happy path test proves sandbox can run a Testcontainers-based check through PSP
- Failure path test proves policy denial surfaces cleanly to AGS user
- Tests document required local preconditions
close_reason: done

title: Document AGS PSP mode usage and boundaries
description:
Goal
Explain how AGS users enable and operate PSP mode.

Acceptance Criteria
- Docs describe setup, flags/config, and troubleshooting
- Security boundaries and responsibility split (AGS vs PSP) are explicit
- Migration path from raw-socket experiments is included
close_reason: done

title: Add dcg guard visibility and yolo override for Pi/Claude
description:
Goal
Make missing dcg visible and operator-controllable now that AGS relies on it for bash classification, and add an explicit `--yolo` escape hatch that disables AGS guard layers for Pi and Claude.

Scope
- Make `ags doctor` report whether `dcg` is present/usable in the sandbox image
- Surface a startup warning when guard integrations are running without `dcg` available
- Add `--yolo` runtime flag to disable Pi and Claude guards entirely
- Wire the flag through launch planning/env/agent setup and document behavior
- Add/update tests where practical

Acceptance Criteria
- Operators can see from `ags doctor` whether dcg is available in the sandbox
- Pi/Claude sessions visibly warn when AGS bash guard delegation would fail open because dcg is missing
- `ags --agent pi --yolo` and `ags --agent claude --yolo` disable AGS guard integrations for that run
- Docs describe the warning/override behavior clearly
notes:
Started implementation. Need to wire three things together: (1) doctor/runtime visibility that dcg is present in the image, (2) a startup warning path when Pi/Claude guards will fail open because dcg is unavailable, and (3) a `--yolo` run flag that disables Pi/Claude guard integrations entirely for that session.

Implementation summary
- Added `--yolo` as an AGS run flag and documented it in CLI/docs.
- Launch planning now carries guard enablement explicitly; Pi/Claude profiles can be built with AGS guards disabled for a run.
- Pi guard extension now respects `AGS_GUARD_YOLO=1`, caches dcg availability, shows an in-session warning if dcg is missing, and exposes dcg state in `/guard`.
- Claude guard hook now exits immediately when `AGS_GUARD_YOLO=1`.
- `ags doctor` now verifies whether bundled `dcg` is available inside the sandbox image and fails if the image exists but dcg is missing.
- Runtime startup now warns before Pi/Claude launches if dcg is unavailable and AGS Bash guard delegation would fail open; `--yolo` prints an explicit guard-disabled warning instead.
- Added regression coverage for CLI parsing, agent profile wiring, yolo env injection, and guard no-op behavior.
- Validation completed with `cargo test -p ags`.
close_reason: Added dcg visibility plus yolo override: doctor now verifies bundled dcg, Pi/Claude launches warn when dcg would make Bash guards fail open, and --yolo disables AGS Pi/Claude guard integrations for a single run with regression coverage.

title: Document container networking for host service access
description: Ensure sandboxed agents can discover host-service routing at runtime, not only in docs. Add explicit runtime signal (env var and startup hint) that host services are reachable via host.containers.internal and localhost is container-local.
close_reason: Implemented runtime host-service discoverability in sandbox sessions (not docs-only): ags now injects AGS_HOST_SERVICES_HOST/AGS_HOST_SERVICES_HINT env vars and prints a startup hint before agent exec. Updated README/COMMANDS/TROUBLESHOOTING and added plan_build tests for env + entrypoint hint.

title: Add ephemeral auth proxy for sandbox browser opens and loopback callbacks
description:
Objective
Provide an explicit, temporary bridge for sandboxed tools that need to open host-browser URLs and optionally receive loopback HTTP callbacks back inside the container.

Scope
- Per-run mounted auth runtime directory with Unix socket path available in the sandbox
- Prompt-based allow/deny flow for browser opens on the host
- Full HTTP callback relay for loopback callback URIs
- Unmanaged open path for URLs without a detectable callback

Out of Scope
- Permanent host daemon or permanent OS URL handler registration
- Custom URI scheme callbacks
- https://localhost callbacks that require trusted local certificates
- Best-effort support for tools that bypass xdg-open/BROWSER interception entirely
close_reason: Ephemeral auth proxy fully implemented with host proxy, container shim, AGS wiring, and tests

title: Specify auth proxy contract, prompt UX, and unsupported flows
description:
Goal
Write the implementation spec for the temporary auth proxy before code lands.

Acceptance Criteria
- Define the host/container message protocol for URL open, callback request relay, response relay, errors, and cancellation
- Define prompt UX for callback-aware Allow/Deny and callback-less Open/Cancel flows
- Define socket discovery and retry behavior when proxy/container startup order varies
- List unsupported flows and failure modes explicitly
close_reason: Protocol spec defined in protocol.rs with ShimMessage and HostMessage types

title: Implement host auth proxy with allow/deny prompt and loopback callback capture
description:
Goal
Run a small host-side proxy that receives URL-open requests from the sandbox, prompts the user, opens the browser on approval, and optionally captures loopback callbacks.

Acceptance Criteria
- Listen on a Unix socket inside the mounted runtime dir
- Show a minimal allow/deny prompt for callback-aware flows and open/cancel prompt otherwise
- Bind the host loopback callback listener before opening the browser
- Relay the full HTTP request/response for approved callback flows
- Clean up one-shot sessions on success, deny, timeout, or disconnect
close_reason: Host proxy implemented in auth_proxy/host.rs with zenity/kdialog prompt and callback capture

title: Wire AGS to mount a per-run auth proxy runtime dir into the sandbox
description:
Goal
Make the auth proxy runtime path available to the sandbox without requiring a fixed startup order.

Acceptance Criteria
- AGS creates a per-run temp directory on the host and bind-mounts it into the container at a stable path
- Container env/config points shims at the expected socket path inside that directory
- Mounting the directory works whether the host proxy starts before or after the container
- Runtime dir is removed when the AGS run exits
close_reason: Per-run runtime dir created in main.rs, mounted at /run/ags-auth-proxy with shim

title: Add sandbox URL-open shim and callback relay for auth proxy sessions
description:
Goal
Intercept sandbox browser-open requests and replay approved callbacks into the local listener inside the container.

Acceptance Criteria
- Provide a simple xdg-open/BROWSER-compatible path for sandbox tools to request host browser opens
- Retry socket discovery briefly, then fail closed with a clear message when the host proxy is unavailable
- For approved callback sessions, replay the full HTTP request to the intended localhost target inside the container and return the response to the host proxy
- Handle multiple sessions with explicit session/request IDs
close_reason: Python shim at agent/auth-proxy-shim handles xdg-open replacement and callback relay

title: Add AGS integration tests for auth proxy open and callback flows
description:
Goal
Cover the temporary auth proxy behavior with automated tests.

Acceptance Criteria
- Test callback-less URL open approval/cancel behavior
- Test end-to-end loopback callback replay from host proxy to container listener
- Test clear failure when the proxy socket is absent
- Test timeout/cleanup behavior for abandoned sessions
close_reason: 11 integration tests covering protocol, allow/deny, callback e2e, concurrency, cleanup

title: Document auth proxy startup, UX, security boundaries, and troubleshooting
description:
Goal
Document how users enable and operate the temporary auth proxy.

Acceptance Criteria
- Document how to start the host proxy and what happens when it is not running
- Document prompt semantics for managed callback vs unmanaged open flows
- Document security boundaries, explicit unsupported cases, and failure modes
- Update README/config/troubleshooting docs as needed
close_reason: Documented auth proxy startup, UX, security boundaries, and troubleshooting across README.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, COMMANDS.md, and TROUBLESHOOTING.md

title: Remove --no-extensions flag from pi launch
description: Stop passing the --no-extensions flag when launching the pi agent so extensions can load normally. Update any related tests/docs to match the new launch behavior and verify the default pi sandbox flow still works as expected.
close_reason: Implemented: pi launch no longer passes --no-extensions; updated profile and entrypoint assertions; cargo test -p ags passes

title: Await PSP implementation deliverables from podman-socket-proxy
description: External blocker for AGS PSP work. The PSP implementation is being developed in ../podman-socket-proxy and AGS-side integration work is blocked until the external implementation and contract are ready enough to consume here. Close this when the external repo work is complete/usable.
close_reason: PSP implementation complete in podman-socket-proxy repo

title: Add minimal tmux setup in sandbox container for Claude 'use teams' panes
description:
Goal
Provide tmux inside the sandbox container with a modern, simple default configuration suitable for Claude Code "use teams" pane workflows.

Requested UX
- Keep default prefix Ctrl-b (do not change)
- Enable mouse support
- Improve default ergonomics/appearance without plugins
- Keep configuration intentionally minimal

Scope
- Install tmux in container image
- Add version-controlled tmux config (e.g., .tmux.conf)
- Validate pane/session behavior in sandbox context
- Document usage and any caveats

Acceptance Criteria
- tmux is available in sandbox container
- Config includes mouse support and Ctrl-b prefix
- No external tmux plugin manager dependency
- Basic team pane workflow is documented and tested

Definition of Done
- A new container started from current setup can run tmux with the configured defaults and support team pane workflows
close_reason: Added sandbox tmux support, opt-in --tmux launches, docs/completions updates, and kitty terminfo support.

title: Allow additional directories to be mounted
description: Add support for configuring and mounting additional host directories into the sandbox beyond the current defaults. Define the config surface, validation rules, and launch-plan/runtime wiring so users can expose extra directories when needed.
close_reason: Implemented support for appending additional directory mounts via ags install --add-dir-mount, with tests and docs.

title: Support repo-local AGS config in PROJECT_ROOT/.ags/config.toml
description: Allow AGS to load an optional repository-local config file from PROJECT_ROOT/.ags/config.toml in addition to the existing user/global config. This must work correctly with git worktrees: resolve PROJECT_ROOT from the active worktree root, not the shared git dir/common metadata. Local config should be able to override existing config fields and also contribute additional mounts instead of only replacing the full mount set.
acceptance_criteria: AGS discovers optional repo-local config at PROJECT_ROOT/.ags/config.toml; repo root resolution works for normal git repos and git worktrees; local config can override existing scalar/object fields from the base config; local config can add additional mounts on top of inherited mounts; merge behavior is documented clearly enough to predict effective config; clear precedence order exists between global/user config and repo-local config
close_reason: Completed: repo-local .ags/config.toml overlay support added with worktree-aware repo root detection, additive repeatable sections, tests, and docs

title: Enable sandbox-safe Glimpse host UI bridge for webviews
description:
Objective
Let agents running inside AGS open host-owned Glimpse webviews without breaking sandbox isolation.

Chosen Direction
Use a host-owned UI service with a daemon-friendly Unix socket protocol. For MVP, AGS starts and stops the service per session and mounts only the dedicated socket/runtime directory into the sandbox. Glimpse client code keeps the same open()/prompt() API and selects direct vs socket-backed transport at runtime.

Success Criteria
- The same high-level Glimpse API works both on host and inside AGS.
- A sandboxed session can request a host-owned prompt/window through a mounted socket without direct access to host display/session primitives.
- Host window lifecycle is owned outside the sandbox and cleaned up when the AGS session ends.
- The contract is versioned, documented, and covered by automated tests across AGS, Glimpse, and glimpse_rust.
- The design leaves room for a future always-on daemon without requiring one for MVP.

Scope in this repo
- AGS lifecycle, mount, env, doctor/setup wiring, and integration tests
- Cross-repo contract tracking for ../glimpse and ../glimpse_rust
- Operator documentation for sandboxed host UI flows

Out of Scope
- A long-lived always-on system daemon
- Non-AGS consumers beyond the documented protocol
- Arbitrary host desktop automation beyond Glimpse windows/prompts
- Falling back to mounting host display/session sockets directly

Relationship to existing work
- Reuse the auth_proxy and PSP sidecar patterns for startup, socket mounts, readiness checks, and cleanup where that fits.
- This is broader than the existing notify-only proxy idea; revisit the older notification-proxy issues once the RFC chooses the final direction.
close_reason: Completed RFC, host UI service, process renderer adapter, AGS integration, sandbox app origin relay, tests, and docs across agent-sandbox/glimpse/glimpse_rust

title: [design] Write cross-repo host UI bridge RFC and socket protocol
description:
Write the cross-repo RFC/ADR that locks the MVP architecture before code lands.

Cover:
- runtime decision model: direct host backend vs socket-backed backend
- why sandbox detection lives above the renderer binary
- socket ownership/lifecycle (per-session service, idle shutdown, future daemon compatibility)
- request/response/event schema for open, prompt, update, close, ready, message, closed, and error
- session/window identifiers, prompt semantics, payload limits, and structured errors
- env vars and discovery paths injected by AGS
- security boundaries and explicitly disallowed capabilities
- versioning/compatibility story between AGS, Glimpse, and glimpse_rust

Deliverable: a committed spec that all three repos can implement against without guessing.
close_reason: RFC written in docs/GLIMPSE_HOST_UI_BRIDGE.md

title: [ags] Implement a session-scoped relay for host webviews to reach sandbox-local apps
description:
Implement the AGS side of sandbox-origin bridging for host-owned webviews.

Scope:
- create a session-scoped relay/reverse-proxy path for sandbox-local temporary web servers
- expose only the relayed origin to the host webview, not generic container networking
- support at least HTTP and WebSocket transport if required by the design
- ensure the relay is scoped to the AGS session, randomized, and cleaned up on exit
- integrate lifecycle management with the host UI service/runtime wiring

This must avoid broad host/container network exposure and should follow the same least-privilege philosophy as auth_proxy and PSP.
close_reason: Implemented AGS session-scoped webview origin relay, sandbox helper, env injection, and plan tests

title: [glimpse] Add sandbox-origin handoff for webview apps served from temporary local servers
description:
Extend ../glimpse so callers can launch host-owned webviews backed by sandbox-local app servers.

Scope:
- support the agreed handoff mechanism for a relayed base URL / origin
- preserve existing direct inline-HTML flows
- define how open()/prompt() callers pass a served-app entrypoint versus raw HTML
- ensure any URL rewriting/origin metadata is compatible with the host UI protocol
- keep the API understandable for callers like pi-interview that currently expect a temporary localhost server

The result should make sandbox-hosted app servers a first-class, explicit mode rather than an accidental localhost assumption.
close_reason: Added Glimpse openURL/loadURL support and resolveSandboxAppURL helper for served-app flows

title: [testing] Cover relayed sandbox app origins for host-owned webviews
description:
Add test coverage for the temporary-server case.

Scope:
- AGS tests for relay startup, random origin assignment, cleanup, and failure modes
- client tests proving a host-owned webview can target a sandbox-backed relayed origin
- protocol/service tests for any origin metadata or auth tokens introduced by the design
- coverage for WebSocket/SSE behavior if those are part of the selected contract

This closes the gap for tools that rely on a transient local web app rather than static HTML.
close_reason: Added AGS relay unit coverage and Glimpse headless helper/served-app contract tests

title: [glimpse_rust] Create the session-scoped host UI service and protocol core
description:
Create the new ../glimpse_rust project as the host-owned UI service.

Scope:
- Rust workspace/crate bootstrap
- Unix socket server and structured JSON protocol types
- client/session registry and window handle bookkeeping
- service CLI flags (socket path, idle timeout, log level, renderer path/config)
- handshake/version reporting and structured errors
- graceful shutdown when no AGS clients remain

Keep the service renderer-agnostic so the macOS adapter and any future daemon mode can reuse the same core.
close_reason: Bootstrapped glimpse_rust host UI service with Unix socket protocol, handshake, CLI flags, session lifecycle, and stub backend tests

title: [glimpse_rust] Implement the macOS renderer adapter for host-owned windows
description:
Implement the macOS adapter in ../glimpse_rust that actually opens host windows.

Scope:
- bridge service-level operations to the current Glimpse renderer protocol / spawned renderer processes
- support open, prompt, update, and close
- relay ready/message/closed/error events back to clients
- clean up child processes and window handles reliably on disconnect/shutdown
- define how renderer binary discovery/config works in dev and production

Do not put sandbox detection in the renderer; the service/host layer owns transport policy.
close_reason: Added process-based renderer adapter in glimpse_rust that proxies the existing Glimpse binary protocol and covers open/update/prompt flows with mock renderer tests

title: [glimpse] Add socket-backed transport while preserving the open()/prompt() API
description:
Update ../glimpse so callers can keep using open()/prompt() regardless of where code runs.

Scope:
- add a transport abstraction with direct and socket-backed backends
- default to socket transport when sandbox env + host UI socket env are present, with explicit override for testing/dev
- preserve EventEmitter semantics and prompt behavior across both backends
- support service-level window IDs and remote event relay
- keep current direct mode working outside AGS

This should make sandboxed code paths invisible to most callers.
close_reason: Added Glimpse transport abstraction with auto/direct/socket modes, socket-backed open/prompt flow, and headless mock-server tests

title: [ags] Start the host UI service, mount its socket, and inject discovery env
description:
Teach AGS to run the host UI service like a sidecar.

Scope:
- start the host UI service before launching the container
- wait for readiness, mount only the dedicated runtime dir/socket into the sandbox, and clean it up on exit
- inject discovery env vars and optional prompt hints for Pi/agent sessions
- make lifecycle behavior match auth_proxy/PSP patterns where it makes sense
- ensure failures are clear when the service cannot start

Do not mount host display/session buses or generic desktop sockets.
close_reason: AGS now starts a session-scoped host UI sidecar, mounts /run/ags-host-ui, injects AGS_HOST_UI_* env vars, and covers startup/plan wiring with tests

title: [ags] Add config, setup/doctor checks, and local-dev wiring for the host UI bridge
description:
Add operator-facing configuration and health checks in AGS.

Scope:
- config fields and env overrides for the host UI service binary path, socket path/name, and optional renderer path
- doctor/setup/update coverage so operators can tell whether the host UI path is usable before starting a session
- clear diagnostics for missing binaries, incompatible platforms, socket start failures, and version mismatches
- local dev workflow for using checkout paths from ../glimpse and ../glimpse_rust

Goal: reduce "works on my machine" drift across the three repos.
close_reason: Added [host_ui] config, wired AGS startup to it, added doctor/setup checks and default local-dev config entries for the host UI bridge

title: [testing] Add protocol and integration coverage for sandbox-opened host webviews
description:
Add automated coverage across the contract boundary.

Scope:
- protocol/unit tests in ../glimpse_rust
- client transport tests in ../glimpse using a mock socket server
- AGS integration tests for service startup, readiness, env injection, mount wiring, and cleanup
- a fake/mock renderer path so CI does not require a real host webview

Coverage should prove the transport and lifecycle even when native UI smoke tests must stay host-only.
close_reason: Added AGS startup/mount/env tests, glimpse socket transport tests with mock host UI server, and glimpse_rust protocol/backend tests with mock renderer coverage

title: [docs] Document sandboxed Glimpse usage, setup, and troubleshooting across repos
description:
Document how to use and debug sandboxed Glimpse flows once the bridge lands.

Update:
- AGS docs for runtime behavior, env vars, setup, troubleshooting, and security boundaries
- ../glimpse README/SKILL with sandbox-aware usage guidance and fallback behavior
- developer notes for hacking across AGS + Glimpse + glimpse_rust in one workspace

Include explicit callouts that the host owns the window and that sandboxed code only sees the socket-backed client.
close_reason: Documented host UI bridge/runtime behavior in AGS docs and added sandbox-aware README/SKILL guidance in glimpse

title: [design] Specify how host-owned webviews reach sandbox-local HTTP/WebSocket apps
description:
The current host UI RFC assumes inline HTML or direct message passing, but some consumers (for example pi-interview) spin up a temporary HTTP server inside the sandbox and expect the webview to load assets and make requests against that origin.

Write the design for that gap.

Cover:
- whether AGS should provide a session-scoped reverse proxy / tunnel / origin relay
- how host-owned webviews discover the relayed origin for a sandbox-local server
- support requirements for HTTP, SSE, and WebSocket traffic
- origin and CSRF/security boundaries for exposing a sandbox server to a host-owned webview
- session scoping, randomization, auth tokens/capabilities, and idle cleanup
- whether the preferred model is explicit base URL handoff, URL rewriting, or a stricter fetch bridge
- when apps should inline HTML instead of relying on a temporary server

Deliverable: a written contract that AGS, Glimpse, and glimpse_rust can implement without inventing their own origin-bridging behavior.
close_reason: Wrote docs/GLIMPSE_SANDBOX_APP_ORIGIN_RELAY.md and updated docs/GLIMPSE_HOST_UI_BRIDGE.md

title: Implement policy-gated Testcontainers broker for sandbox (no raw podman socket mount)
description:
Objective
Enable agents inside the sandbox container to run Testcontainers-based integration tests without directly mounting the host Podman socket into the sandbox.

Chosen Direction
Adopt a broker/proxy architecture (Option 2): sandbox talks to a restricted Docker-compatible broker endpoint; broker talks to rootless host Podman API and enforces policy.

Problem to Solve
Raw socket sharing works functionally but breaks sandbox isolation because commands can create arbitrary containers/mounts as the host user.

Success Criteria
- Testcontainers workflows run from inside sandbox using broker endpoint
- Broker enforces explicit policy on container/network/volume operations
- Dangerous operations are blocked with explainable errors
- Session lifecycle and cleanup are reliable
- Security model and trade-offs are documented

Out of Scope (for this epic)
- Remote/CI backend support
- Windows/macOS runtime parity beyond documented caveats
- Full multi-tenant authorization model
close_reason: Moved to standalone PSP project in ../podman-socket-proxy (epic psp-287)

title: Write broker architecture decision record and threat model
description:
Goal
Produce an ADR that fixes architecture boundaries before coding.

Scope
- Document trust boundaries: sandbox container, broker process, host Podman API
- Enumerate abuse cases (arbitrary bind mounts, privileged flags, host namespace escape attempts)
- Decide broker deployment model (ephemeral per `ags` run vs long-lived daemon)
- Define minimal supported Docker API surface for v1
- Define default deny-first policy posture

Deliverables
- docs/adr/xxxx-testcontainers-broker.md
- Threat model table with mitigations and residual risk
- Explicit non-goals and known limitations

Acceptance Criteria
- ADR reviewed and linked from docs/ARCHITECTURE.md
- Includes decision rationale against raw socket mounting
- Includes implementation constraints that downstream tasks can follow without ambiguity
close_reason: Moved to standalone PSP project in ../podman-socket-proxy (epic psp-287)

title: Implement broker MVP: Docker-compatible API fronting rootless Podman socket
description:
Goal
Build a broker service that accepts a constrained Docker-compatible API from sandbox and forwards allowed calls to rootless Podman API on host.

Scope
- Stand up broker process with Unix socket endpoint dedicated to sandbox session
- Forward core endpoints needed by Testcontainers flows (container create/start/inspect/logs/stop/rm, image pull/inspect, network create/inspect/rm)
- Normalize Podman/Docker response differences where required for clients
- Add robust request/response error mapping

Acceptance Criteria
- Broker can run a minimal create/start/wait/logs/remove container flow end-to-end
- Endpoint compatibility validated with a smoke client using Docker API calls
- Broker can connect to host rootless Podman socket without requiring privileged container flags
- Unsupported endpoints fail with explicit, structured errors
close_reason: Moved to standalone PSP project in ../podman-socket-proxy (epic psp-287)

title: Add policy engine to broker (deny dangerous container options by default)
description:
Goal
Enforce command-level safety controls in broker before forwarding requests to Podman.

Policy v1
- Deny privileged mode, host PID/IPC/network namespaces
- Deny host bind mounts unless path matches allowlist
- Deny device mounts and sensitive capabilities additions
- Restrict image sources/tags via allowlist or policy rules
- Enforce resource limits (pids/cpu/memory) where feasible

Scope
- Parse relevant Docker API payload fields for create/run/network operations
- Implement policy evaluation and structured deny reasons
- Provide version-controlled default policy file + schema validation

Acceptance Criteria
- Blocked requests return actionable reason + policy rule identifier
- Allow/deny behavior covered by automated unit tests
- Default policy prevents raw host filesystem escalation patterns
- Policy overrides are explicit and auditable
close_reason: Moved to standalone PSP project in ../podman-socket-proxy (epic psp-287)

title: Integrate brokered Testcontainers mode into ags CLI/config
description:
Goal
Expose brokered Testcontainers mode in AGS as an explicit, opt-in workflow.

Scope
- Add config section for broker mode (enabled flag, policy path, socket path, optional image allowlist override)
- Add run-time wiring so sandbox sees broker endpoint as Docker host
- Ensure default AGS mode remains unchanged (no broker unless enabled)
- Add prominent warning/help text describing changed security boundary

Acceptance Criteria
- `ags` can launch sandbox with broker mode enabled via config/flag
- Sandbox environment is configured to use broker endpoint (`DOCKER_HOST`)
- Raw host Podman socket is not directly mounted into sandbox in broker mode
- Misconfiguration errors are clear and actionable
close_reason: Moved to standalone PSP project in ../podman-socket-proxy (epic psp-287)

title: Solve in-sandbox host/port resolution for Testcontainers networking
description:
Goal
Make tests running inside sandbox reliably reach published container ports created through broker/Podman.

Scope
- Validate host mapping behavior for Testcontainers on Podman API from inside container
- Implement required host override strategy (env/properties/network mode)
- Validate compatibility with random host ports and parallel test runs
- Document known caveats (e.g., localhost assumptions)

Acceptance Criteria
- A reproducible test proves sandbox test process can connect to published ports
- Strategy works with at least one canonical module (e.g., Postgres or Redis)
- No manual per-test host patching required for basic path
- Troubleshooting doc includes hostname/port diagnostics
close_reason: Moved to standalone PSP project in ../podman-socket-proxy (epic psp-287)

title: Implement broker session lifecycle, labeling, and deterministic cleanup
description:
Goal
Ensure resources created via broker are attributable to AGS sessions and cleaned up reliably.

Scope
- Tag created containers/networks/volumes with AGS session metadata
- Implement cleanup on normal exit and crash-recovery sweep on startup
- Add TTL-based garbage collection for orphaned resources
- Support optional keep-on-failure mode for debugging

Acceptance Criteria
- No orphan resource growth in repeated test runs under normal conditions
- Crash simulation leaves resources that are recovered by next cleanup sweep
- Keep-on-failure mode is explicit and documented
- Cleanup behavior is covered by integration tests
close_reason: Moved to standalone PSP project in ../podman-socket-proxy (epic psp-287)

title: Add broker audit logs and operator-visible deny diagnostics
description:
Goal
Make broker decisions observable for debugging and security auditing.

Scope
- Emit structured logs for allow/deny decisions with rule IDs
- Record principal/session context and target operation metadata
- Surface concise deny messages to sandbox user while keeping detailed host logs
- Provide log redaction for sensitive payload fields

Acceptance Criteria
- Denied operations can be traced to specific policy rules
- Logs are machine-parseable and documented
- Sensitive tokens/secrets are not logged in plaintext
- Operator can diagnose common failures from docs + logs alone
close_reason: Moved to standalone PSP project in ../podman-socket-proxy (epic psp-287)

title: Create end-to-end brokered Testcontainers integration test suite
description:
Goal
Prove the full pipeline works: sandbox test process -> broker -> rootless Podman -> test container lifecycle.

Scope
- Add deterministic e2e tests covering happy path and blocked path
- Include at least one real Testcontainers-based integration test in Rust
- Validate parallel run behavior and cleanup guarantees
- Add CI-friendly local test target (even if CI execution deferred)

Acceptance Criteria
- Happy path test starts service container and runs assertion successfully
- Blocked path test verifies policy denial for prohibited mount/flag
- Tests assert no leaked containers/networks after run
- Test commands are documented for contributors
close_reason: Moved to standalone PSP project in ../podman-socket-proxy (epic psp-287)

title: Document brokered Testcontainers usage, security model, and migration path
description:
Goal
Ship user-facing and contributor-facing docs for the new brokered mode.

Scope
- Update README/docs/COMMANDS/docs/CONFIG/docs/TROUBLESHOOTING
- Add security section comparing raw socket mount vs broker mode
- Document setup, enablement flags, policy customization, and common failures
- Provide migration steps from existing raw-socket experiments

Acceptance Criteria
- New mode can be enabled by following docs alone
- Security implications are explicit and non-ambiguous
- Troubleshooting covers hostname/port mapping, denied commands, cleanup issues
- All new config fields are documented with examples
close_reason: Moved to standalone PSP project in ../podman-socket-proxy (epic psp-287)

title: Add update-available notification on CLI startup
description: Check GitHub releases for newer ags versions and show a subtle stderr notice after command output. Cache check results (24h TTL), run check in background, skip on non-TTY/piped output, skip on update-self/--version/completions commands.
close_reason: Implemented update check with cache, background refresh, and stderr notice

title: Implement host user-session notification proxy
description: Implement a small host-side process that listens on a dedicated Unix socket in the user session and emits desktop notifications locally. Must not expose generic DBus access. Include schema validation, bounds checks, and reasonable failure logging.

title: Wire AGS to mount dedicated notification socket into sandbox
description: Add AGS support for the host notification proxy socket: create or discover the host socket, mount only that socket into the container at a fixed path, and expose the container path to agents/extensions. Do not mount the host session bus.

title: Write spec for host notification proxy contract and security model
description: Nail down the design before implementation. Cover: host socket path and lifecycle, container mount path, payload schema (title/body/urgency/tag), optional per-session token, max payload sizes, rate limiting, allowed operations (notify only), host backend choice (libnotify vs org.freedesktop.Notifications), error semantics, and threat model. Acceptance: a written spec with enough detail to implement and test.

title: Move extra directory mounts to run command flags
description: Fix the mistaken install-time mount flag behavior by supporting additive runtime directory mounts on the main ags run command. Replace the install flag with a run-mode flag named --add-dir / -d, allow repeated usage, and wire the extra directories into launch-plan mounts and roots.
close_reason: Implemented runtime extra directory mounts via --add-dir/-d on ags run, with CLI, launch plan, completions, tests, and docs updates.

title: Add xdg-open localhost proxy choice dialog
description:
When a package/tool falls back to xdg-open/browser-open handling, intercept the flow and present a popup with explicit choices:

1. Cancel
2. OK (open original URL)
3. Proxy

Requirements:
- Proxy option is shown only when the target URL is localhost/127.0.0.1 and includes an explicit port.
- Choosing Proxy should route through the same AGS sandbox-app relay mechanism used for host webviews today (dedicated host port / relay flow), so sandbox-local apps can be reached from the host safely.
- Evaluate whether the popup itself should be rendered via Glimpse and whether Glimpse can directly open the proxied URL after resolution.
- Preserve original behavior for non-localhost URLs and localhost URLs without a port.
- Define UX/contract for tools/packages that call xdg-open indirectly so this choice works consistently across the stack.

Validation:
- Cancel aborts the open request.
- OK opens the original URL.
- Proxy opens the resolved host-reachable URL and preserves path/query/hash.
- Proxy option is absent for non-localhost URLs.
- Document how this interacts with Glimpse-owned URL resolution.
